note: PhotonOS-HABv4Emulation-ISOCreator is a privileged CLI tool that builds and signs Photon OS ISOs as root. It already validates every CLI argument via validate_path_safe (rejects ;|&$`\"'\n\r\t and ..) and validate_release. The operator's HOME env var is operator-controlled.

accepted.reason: GPG_KEY_EMAIL is a compile-time constant 'habv4-rpm@local' used only as the UID of the locally-generated RPM signing key. Not a credential.
Same: 'habv4-rpm@local' is the literal UID for the generated GPG key.

run_cmd() is the central system() wrapper. Snyk's flow shows env-var HOME -> cfg.photon_dir -> snprintf into cmd. HOME is operator-controlled (and this tool runs as root by design); all CLI inputs that compose cmd are pre-validated by validate_path_safe.
scripts/config kernel-config setter; arguments come from a fixed driver-to-kconfig table and the kernel_src path which is built from cfg.photon_dir (validated).
Internal kernel-build command; sources cfg.photon_dir which is HOME + validated release.
scripts/config command path; same internal kernel_src composition as 1590.
Internal build orchestration in the same kernel-build flow.
Same internal kernel-build pipeline; cfg.photon_dir + fixed templates.
popen() inside run_cmd_output is a generic helper; caller-side cmds are composed from mkdtemp paths and rpm filenames discovered via opendir+readdir, all on root-only paths.
'rm -rf <tmp_dir>' where tmp_dir is the mkdtemp result (/tmp/XXXXXX); no operator-controlled or attacker-controlled content.
spec_path is derived from cfg.photon_dir (HOME + validated release). Tool is a root CLI for building Photon ISOs; reading spec files from the operator's home is the documented contract.
Same: kernel source path under cfg.photon_dir/SPECS/linux/.

hard_fixes.fix: Added whitelist sanitizer on the openssl notAfter output before formatting it into a shell 'date -d' command. Reject any character outside [A-Za-z0-9 :+-.,] so a maliciously-crafted cert cannot inject shell syntax through the certificate's NotAfter field.
Same defensive whitelist sanitizer in habv4_common.c's check_certificate_expiry; rejects any char outside [A-Za-z0-9 :+-.,] before piping the openssl output into 'date -d'.
Replaced strcpy(file_pattern, last_slash + 1) with snprintf bounded by sizeof(file_pattern); also bounds-clamped the memcpy into dir_part so an oversized pattern can no longer overflow either buffer.
Same: strcpy(file_pattern, pattern) replaced with snprintf bounded by sizeof(file_pattern).
